topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, interest rates, and borrower information related to federal student loans.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require that when the holder of a borrower's loans under the Federal Family Education Loan (FFEL) or William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan (DL) programs receives a prepayment for such loans, it is to be applied first toward any fees due on such loans
Requires an institution of higher education holding a borrower's loans under the Federal Perkins Loan program to first apply any excess payments by the borrower toward any fees due on such loans.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the annual interest rate on Direct Loans at the bond equivalent rate on 91-day Treasury bills

Requires institutions of higher education (IHEs) to provide student borrowers of title IV loans, prior to or at the time of their departure from school, with information regarding their options for loan consolidation.
Document 3: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand lender disclosure requirements
Additionally, the legislation modifies loan counseling requirements for an institution of higher education (IHE) that participates in federal student aid programs.

topic.name: Labor Relations and Elections
topic.description: Legislation governing the processes and requirements for conducting elections related to collective bargaining representation in the workplace.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition regarding collective bargaining representation.
Requires the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition regarding collective bargaining representation.
Requires the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees of an appropriate unit, whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.

topic.name: Electric Vehicle Policy and Deployment
topic.description: The documents focus on federal policies and programs aimed at promoting the deployment of electric vehicles and reducing oil consumption in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes in the Executive Office of the President a national energy security program to coordinate federal activities and policies to reduce oil consumption by 8 million barrels per day by calendar 2030.
Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national electric drive vehicle deployment program.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate joint regulations establishing fuel efficiency standards and greenhouse gas emissions limitations.
Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program.
Document 3: Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national electric drive vehicle deployment program.
Allows a refundable personal credit for a qualified deployment community taxpayer.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reducing improper payments and fraud in Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Document 1: Preventing and Reducing Improper Medicare and Medicaid Expenditures Act of 2015
Requires the Secretary's annual report to Congress on the use of recovery audit contractors under the Medicare Integrity Program
Document 2: Preventing and Reducing Improper Medicare and Medicaid Expenditures Act of 2013
Requires the Secretary to address improper payment vulnerabilities in a timely manner
Document 3: Amends part D (Prescription Drug Benefits) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Directs the Secretary to develop a plan to revise the incentive program under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 for the reporting of fraud and abuse

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing criminal penalties and addressing violent crime, gang activity, and drug trafficking.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to increase or impose new criminal penalties for the use of firearms to commit a crime of violence or a drug trafficking crime.
Imposes or increases criminal penalties for certain crimes of terrorism.
Document 2: Extends the limitation period for prosecuting violent crimes and crimes of terrorism.
Increases criminal penalties for sexual exploitation of children and child pornography.
Document 3: Defines 'criminal street gang' and 'gang crime' and prohibits the commission of gang crimes.
Increases criminal penalties for possession of firearms by repeat criminal offenders.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions and Nuclear Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran to prevent its development of nuclear weapons and to address its support for terrorism.

Document 1: U.S. policy to prevent Iran from acquiring or developing nuclear weapons
implement all sanctions against Iran to compel it to abandon nuclear weapons efforts
Document 2: international diplomatic efforts to address Iran's illicit nuclear efforts are more likely to be effective if strong additional sanctions are imposed
the September 2009 revelation that Iran is developing a secret uranium enrichment site highlights the urgency for Iran to disclose its nuclear program
Document 3: compelling Iran to abandon efforts to acquire a nuclear weapons capability can be achieved through a policy that includes economic sanctions
increase the minimum number of sanctions that the President is required to impose for violations relating to development of Iran's petroleum resources

topic.name: Tariff Suspension on Chemicals
topic.description: This topic covers the legislative amendments related to the suspension of duties on specific chemical compounds under the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on a compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
suspension of duty on a compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
temporary suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.

topic.name: Aviation Policy and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and initiatives aimed at improving aviation safety, security, and operational efficiency in the United States.

Document 1: reauthorizes the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
establishes the FAA Task Force on Flight Standards Reform
Document 2: extends funding for aviation security
develop a system to screen all cargo transported on domestic and foreign passenger airlines
Document 3: establish a pilot program under which DOT shall install, operate, and assess the benefits of the technology of up to six remote air traffic control towers
DOT must complete a safety risk management process for a participating airport

topic.name: U.S. Agricultural Commodity Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative frameworks governing commodity payments and adjustments for U.S. farmers under various agricultural acts.

Document 1: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to notify farm owners regarding their opportunity to elect to retain base acres
Document 2: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs
Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments or conservation payments

topic.name: Retirement Savings Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying tax codes to enhance retirement savings options and benefits for various demographics.

Document 1: establish a tax-exempt individual retirement account for taxpayers under age 18
tax credit for retirement savings for contributions to a young savers account
Document 2: suspend in 2008 and 2009 requirements for minimum distributions from tax-deferred retirement plans
recontribute such distribution to their retirement plans
Document 3: taxpayers who do not itemize their deductions to deduct from gross income the costs of health insurance
payments from health savings accounts for non-group high deductible health plan premiums

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Amendments on Textiles
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific synthetic and rayon staple fibers, as well as provisions for duty-free treatment of certain imported textiles.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers
not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on staple fibers of viscose rayon
measuring over 1.3 decitex but not over 1.66 decitex
Document 3: Extends duty-free treatment for certain imported Nicaraguan woven trousers
accompanied by an earned import allowance certificate

topic.name: Water Pollution Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss regulations and provisions related to water pollution control, specifically focusing on the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System permits and pipeline safety in relation to environmental risks.

Document 1: prohibited from requiring a National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System permit under the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
Ends the prohibition on December 18, 2015
Document 2: prohibits the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from requiring a National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System permit
This prohibition does not apply with respect to garbage discharged overboard
Document 3: conduct a study to determine the economic and environmental risks of oil or hazardous liquids spills
evaluate the condition and structural integrity of the facilities

topic.name: Emergency Unemployment Compensation
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and extensions related to emergency unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
amends the Federal-State Extended Unemployment Compensation Act of 1970
Document 2: revises the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation
Document 3: requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA
increases the figures in the basic EUC formula

topic.name: Tax Deductions for Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses various tax relief measures aimed at supporting education-related expenses for teachers, students, and their families.

Document 1: tax deduction for certain expenses of elementary and secondary school teachers
expanded tax deduction for charitable contributions of computer technology or equipment to schools
Document 2: increase the maximum tax deduction for interest paid on any qualified education loan
repeal the limitation on such deduction based upon modified adjusted gross income
Document 3: make permanent the tax deduction for qualified tuition and related expenses
allow grandparents to claim a deduction for the tuition and related expenses of their grandchildren

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Native Tribes
topic.description: This topic encompasses the process and implications of extending federal recognition to Native American tribes, including eligibility for services and benefits.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States to Indians.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States to Indians.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided to Indians because of their status as members of a federally recognized tribe.

topic.name: Legislative Actions on Public Land and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to public land management and educational initiatives for children.

Document 1: Permits statues honoring deceased citizens of U.S. territories to be placed in Statuary Hall of the U.S. Capitol.
Limits such statues to one per jurisdiction.
Document 2: Modifies the boundary of Minute Man National Historical Park in Massachusetts to include the Colonel James Barrett Farm in Concord.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to study and report to Congress on methods for identifying and treating children with dyslexia in kindergarten through third grade.

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer protections in credit transactions and debt settlement practices.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information about a consumer credit transaction.
Modifies the inclusion in the computation of points and fees of all compensation paid to mortgage brokers.
Document 2: Amends the Consumer Credit Protection Act to prohibit debt settlement providers from providing a debt settlement service or receiving a fee from a consumer without a signed written contract.
Prohibits debt settlement providers from engaging in certain acts or practices, including making loans or offering credit.

topic.name: Political Robocalls Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating and restricting politically-oriented robocalls to protect consumer privacy.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Trade Commission to revise the do-not-call registry provisions of the Telemarketing Sales Rule to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls.

Defines a 'politically-oriented recorded message telephone call' as an outbound telephone call that plays a recorded message that promotes, advertises, campaigns, or solicits donations for or against a political candidate.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions of the Telemarketing Sales Rule to prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls.

Defines a 'politically-oriented recorded message telephone call' as an outbound telephone call that plays a recorded message to promote, advertise, campaign, or solicit donations for or against any political candidate.

topic.name: Transportation Regulations and Standards
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and regulatory standards aimed at improving various aspects of transportation, particularly in aviation and navigation systems.

Document 1: prohibit a air carrier charging passengers a fee for checked baggage
size standard for carry-on baggage
Document 2: require each air carrier to develop and implement standards
proper qualifications and experience to serve as crewmembers
Document 3: Redesignates Lock and Dam No. 5
authorized by the Rivers and Harbors Act

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization Acts
topic.description: The documents discuss the authorization of appropriations for various intelligence and intelligence-related activities for fiscal years 2014 and 2017.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2014 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings...
Document 2: This bill authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
The amounts authorized, and the authorized personnel ceilings, are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations...
Document 3: This bill authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
The ODNI must establish guidelines to govern the treatment under such authorized personnel levels...

topic.name: STEM Immigration Visa Policies
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing immigration pathways for skilled workers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) fields.

Document 1: exempt from the annual H-1B visa cap an alien who has earned a master's or higher degree in science, technology, engineering, or mathematics
annual limits at: 115,000 for FY2007
Document 2: make up to 55,000 visas available to qualified immigrants who possess a graduate degree at the level of master's or higher in a field of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics
have an employment offer from a U.S. employer in a field related to such degree
Document 3: make up to 50,000 visas available to qualified immigrants who possess a graduate degree at the level of master's or higher in a field of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics
eliminates the foreign residency requirement for certain foreign students

topic.name: Tenth Amendment Regulatory Reform
topic.description: Legislation that allows state officials to challenge the constitutionality of federal rules under the Tenth Amendment.

Document 1: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, during the period when the proposed rule is required to be open for public comment, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Directs the agency head to notify the designated official of each state within 15 days after such a brief is filed.
Document 2: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, during the period when the proposed rule is required to be open for public comment, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Directs the agency head to post prominently on the agency's primary Web page a link to the brief.
Document 3: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, during the period when the proposed rule is required to be open for public comment, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment.
Directs the relevant U.S. Court of Appeals, at the request of a designated state official, to grant expedited review of a decision by a district court in such a case.

topic.name: Energy Tax Incentives
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts related to tax incentives and credits for renewable energy production and energy efficiency improvements.

Document 1: repeal the excise tax credits for alcohol fuel, biodiesel, and alternative fuel mixtures
tax credits for the production of electricity from certain renewable resources
Document 2: extends the tax credits for producing electricity from certain renewable resources
expands the tax credit for production of electricity from renewable resources
Document 3: amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to renewable energy and other energy-related tax incentives
extends eligibility for clean renewable energy bonds

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and opportunities for small businesses through various programs and funding mechanisms.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to reduce state matching funds requirements
requires a report from the SBA Administrator to the congressional small business committees
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the HUBZone program
authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish mentor-protege programs for small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans and women
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act relating to the women's business centers program
requires the SBA to establish center performance measures

topic.name: Commercial Privacy Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting individuals' personal information through security measures and transparency requirements for entities that collect and use such data.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to initiate a rulemaking to require covered entities that collect or use information about individuals to carry out security measures to protect personally identifiable information.
Defines 'covered entity' as a person... who collects, uses, transfers, or stores such information concerning more than 5,000 individuals during any consecutive 12-month period.
Document 2: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to initiate a rulemaking to require covered entities that collect or use information about individuals to carry out security measures to protect personally identifiable information.
Defines 'covered entity' as a person... who collects, uses, transfers, or stores such information concerning more than 5,000 individuals during any consecutive 12-month period.
Document 3: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to initiate a rulemaking to require covered entities to carry out security measures to protect personally identifiable information.
Defines 'covered entity' as a person... who collects, uses, transfers, or stores such information concerning more than 5,000 individuals during any consecutive 12-month period.

topic.name: Small Business Regulatory Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving the regulatory environment and contracting opportunities for small businesses.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to consolidate Small Business Administration (SBA) provisions relating to contract bundling
Requires the head of an agency, before proceeding with an acquisition strategy that could lead to a bundling of contract requirements, to conduct market research
Document 2: Revises the Regulatory Flexibility Act
Requires initial regulatory flexibility analyses of a proposed rule to contain a detailed statement describing the type of small entities to which the proposed rule will apply
Document 3: Amends the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 to require the Director of the Congressional Budget Office (CBO) to include estimates of the potential job creation or job loss
Requires each agency to place on its website its plan for the periodic review of rules, providing for the review of all agency rules at specified intervals

topic.name: Mental Health Care for Military Personnel
topic.description: The documents focus on the importance of mental health care for military personnel and veterans, emphasizing the need for access to treatment, research, and workforce development.

Document 1: members of the Armed Forces deserve the best possible treatment for mental and physical illnesses
there is a continued need for research, new treatments, and best practices for treating PTSD
Document 2: members of the Armed Forces deserve the best possible treatment for mental and physical illnesses
mental health disorders such as post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) should be treated with an urgency similar to physical ailments
Document 3: expand materially and substantially the scope of research and education on, and delivery of holistic care
assess the feasibility and advisability of establishing not fewer than 10 complementary and alternative medicine centers within VA medical centers

topic.name: Advanced Vehicle Technologies
topic.description: Research and development of innovative technologies aimed at reducing petroleum use and emissions in vehicles.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a program of basic and applied research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes with the potential to substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use by, and emissions from, the nation's passenger and commercial vehicles.
Requires the program to include activities in the areas of: hybridization or full electrification of vehicle systems; batteries, ultracapacitors, and other energy storage devices.
Document 2: Advanced energy technologies are innovative technologies or research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities necessary to ensure the long-term, secure, and sustainable supply of energy critical elements.
Enables advanced vehicles, vehicle components, and related technologies that result in significant energy savings.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a program of basic and applied research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes with the potential to substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use by, and emissions from, the nation's passenger and commercial vehicles.
Requires the program to include activities in the areas of: hybridization or full electrification of vehicle systems; batteries, ultracapacitors, and other energy storage devices.

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, withdrawal, and conveyance of federal lands by the Bureau of Land Management and the Secretary of the Interior.

Document 1: Withdraws the approximately 1,068,908 acres of the Tusayan Ranger District and federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM)
Subjects such withdrawal to valid, existing rights.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Park City, Utah, two parcels of property located in Park City that are currently under the management jurisdiction of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM)
offer for sale two specified parcels of property located in Park City that are currently under the management jurisdiction of the BLM.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Mississippi, by quitclaim deed and without consideration, all interest of the United States in two parcels of identified land
Adjusts the boundary of the Parkway to exclude the conveyed land and provides for inclusion in the Parkway of the approximately 10 acres identified as the proposed addition.

topic.name: Environmental and Cybersecurity Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on enhancing environmental protection through green infrastructure and improving cybersecurity measures for critical information systems.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide competitive grants to establish and maintain centers of excellence for green infrastructure.
Defines 'green infrastructure' as a stormwater management technique that preserves, restores, enhances, or mimics natural hydrology.
Document 2: Defines 'cybersecurity' as 'information security' which is defined as protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access.
Requires the President to initiate a rulemaking to establish a procedure for the designation of critical infrastructure information systems.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax deductions and credits.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the period in which a corporation may carryover excess contributions
tax deduction for charitable contributions from 5 to 20 succeeding taxable years
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax deduction for income attributable to domestic production activities
in Puerto Rico
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the restrictions on the tax deduction for the travel expenses
of a taxpayer's spouse, dependent, or other individual accompanying the taxpayer on business travel

topic.name: Defense Authorization Acts
topic.description: Legislation authorizing appropriations and procurement for the Department of Defense and Coast Guard operations.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles.
Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from procuring more than one Future Combat Systems spin out early-infantry brigade combat team equipment set.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles.
Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from procuring more than two brigade sets of early-infantry brigade combat team increment one equipment.
Document 3: Authorizes FY2011 appropriations for the Coast Guard, including for operation and maintenance, acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of aids to navigation.
Authorizes Coast Guard active duty personnel end-of-year strength for FY2011.

topic.name: Capital Gains and Estate Tax Relief
topic.description: Legislation focused on the reduction of capital gains tax rates and adjustments to estate tax provisions.

Document 1: Makes permanent the reduction in capital gains tax rates (from 20 to 15%)
provide for annual increases in the estate tax exclusion amount between 2010 and 2015
Document 2: Makes permanent the reduction in capital gains tax rates (from 20 to 15%)
establish a permanent exclusion amount of $5 million for 2015 and thereafter
Document 3: establish a permanent $3.5 million estate tax exclusion
restore after 2010 the estate tax deduction for family-owned business interests

topic.name: Breast Cancer Treatment Coverage
topic.description: Legislation ensuring comprehensive health insurance coverage for breast cancer treatments including hospital stays and outpatient services.

Document 1: require a group or individual health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits to ensure that inpatient coverage and radiation therapy are provided for breast cancer treatment
Prohibits such a plan from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay to less than 48 hours in connection with a mastectomy
Document 2: require a group health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits to ensure that inpatient coverage and radiation therapy are provided for breast cancer treatment
Prohibits such a plan from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay to less than 48 hours in connection with a mastectomy
Document 3: require a group health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits to ensure that inpatient coverage and radiation therapy are provided for breast cancer treatment
Prohibits such a plan from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay to less than 48 hours in connection with a mastectomy

topic.name: Wilderness Designation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation and management of wilderness areas across various states in the U.S.

Document 1: Designates as wilderness: certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) lands
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate as wild, scenic, or recreational rivers specified ...
Document 2: Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas
Directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA) and the Department of the Interior to seek to ensure that the wilderness areas are interpreted for the public
Document 3: Designates the following areas in California as wilderness areas
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate segments of the following as components of the national wild and scenic rivers system

topic.name: Fisheries and Marine Ecosystem Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at the conservation and management of fisheries and marine ecosystems, focusing on species recovery, sustainable practices, and the economic needs of fishing communities.

Document 1: Strengthening Fishing Communities and Increasing Flexibility in Fisheries Management Act
Revises requirements for fishery management plans for overfished fisheries.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Interior... to carry out a Recovery and Research Program for southern sea otter populations
implementation of measures to reduce or eliminate potential factors limiting populations that relate to marine ecosystem health or human activities.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior... to carry out a Recovery and Research Program for southern sea otter populations
interactions between southern sea otters and coastal fisheries and other human activities in the marine environment.

topic.name: Small Business Innovation Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on the reauthorization and enhancement of the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs to support small businesses in research and technology development.

Document 1: reauthorize through FY2018 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increases by 0.1% per year the percentage of participating federal agencies' extramural research budget to be set aside for SBIR program activities.
Document 2: reauthorize through FY2019 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increases by 0.1% per year the percentage of participating federal agencies' extramural research budget to be set aside for SBIR program activities.

topic.name: Tax Reform Legislation 2014
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various tax reform measures and legislation introduced in 2014 aimed at modifying individual and business tax provisions.

Document 1: Revises individual income tax rates to establish three tax brackets (10%, 25%, and 35%)
Establishes a single standard deduction of $22,000 for married couples filing jointly and $11,000 for single filers.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2015 expiring tax provisions pertaining to individual and business taxpayers
Expresses the sense of the Senate that a process of comprehensive tax reform should commence in the 114th Congress.

Document 3: Extends through 2014 the tax deduction of expenses of elementary and secondary school teachers
Extends through 2014 the tax credit for increasing research activities.

topic.name: USA FREEDOM Act and FISA Reforms
topic.description: Legislative reforms to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act aimed at regulating the FBI's access to business records and call detail records for foreign intelligence investigations.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process to be followed when the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) submits an application to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of tangible things.
Defines 'call detail record' as session identifying information.
Document 2: Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used as the basis for the production.

Establishes two separate frameworks for the production of tangible things with different standards that apply based on whether the FBI's application seeks production on an ongoing basis of call detail records.

topic.name: Education and Workforce Development
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving education systems to better prepare students for emerging professions and STEM careers through partnerships with various organizations.

Document 1: establish a Connecting Education and Emerging Professions Demonstration Grant program
prepare students for both direct entry into careers with emerging industries
Document 2: establish a Connecting Education and Emerging Professions Demonstration Grant program
prepare students for both direct entry into careers with emerging industries
Document 3: award matching grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to improve science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education
collaborate with institutions of higher education and local, regional, or national employers in STEM fields

topic.name: Rural Water Infrastructure Projects
topic.description: Legislation authorizing federal support for the planning, design, and construction of rural water systems in the United States.

Document 1: conditions the construction by the Secretary of the Interior of the Arkansas Valley Conduit
requires any project rates charged for water for municipal, domestic, or industrial use or for the use of facilities for the storage or delivery of water to be adjusted
Document 2: Authorizes the Department of the Interior to carry out the projects entitled: the 'Dry-Redwater Regional Water Authority System'
sets forth the federal share of such costs and the authorized uses of federal funds, which exclude operation, maintenance, or replacement of the Water Systems
Document 3: authorizes the Department of the Interior to carry out the projects entitled: the 'Dry-Redwater Regional Water Authority System'
Interior must enter into a cooperative agreement with the Dry-Redwater Regional Water Authority and the Central Montana Regional Water Authority

topic.name: Veterans Burial Benefits
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving burial and funeral expense allowances for deceased veterans.

Document 1: Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans
Authorizes the annual adjustment of such allowances by the same percentage increase as adjustments to benefit amounts payable under title II
Document 2: Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for veterans who die as a result of a service-connected disability
Increases the burial plot allowance for veterans who, at the time of death, were receiving hospital or nursing home care in or through the Department of Veterans Affairs
Document 3: Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans
Authorizes the annual adjustment of such allowances by the same percentage increase as adjustments to benefit amounts payable under title II

topic.name: Pension and Retirement Plan Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative modifications and reforms related to pension plans and retirement savings, including technical amendments to existing laws and new provisions for plan sponsors.

Document 1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) and the Internal Revenue Code with respect to the formula for the target normal plan cost element
Makes technical amendments to ERISA and the Internal Revenue Code, as amended by the Preservation of Access to Care for Medicare Beneficiaries and Pension Relief Act of 2010 (PACMBPRA)
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for annuity accumulation retirement plans for state and local government employees

Revises rules relating to pension plan amendments, discrimination testing, restrictions on hardship distributions, rollovers, forfeitures, notice requirements for new plan participants, and plan terminations.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) to repeal the 10% cap on the qualified percentage of an employee's compensation
Revises the period of service requirements for a qualified cash or deferred arrangement to cover long-term part-time employees.

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: The documents discuss the official designation of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 500 North Brevard Avenue in Cocoa Beach, Florida, as the 'Richard K. Salick Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 198 Baker Street in Corning, New York, as the 'Specialist Ryan P. Jayne Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 401 McElroy Drive in Oxford, Mississippi, as the 'Army First Lieutenant Donald C. Carwile Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Trade Adjustment Assistance
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to Trade Adjustment Assistance for workers affected by globalization and trade policies.

Document 1: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to allow the filing of a petition with the Secretary of Labor for certification of eligibility to apply for trade adjustment assistance (TAA) by adversely affected workers in a service sector firm
Revises group eligibility requirements for TAA to cover a shift, by such workers' firm or subdivision to a foreign country, of production of articles or in provision of services.
Document 2: Incentives for Hiring and Retaining Unemployed Workers
Exempt for-profit and nonprofit employers from social security and railroad retirement taxes for new employees who certify that they have not worked more than 40 hours during the last 60 days.
Document 3: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP)
Amends the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985 to increase the customs user fee for the processing of merchandise.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various federal laws aimed at regulating abortion practices, including restrictions based on gestational age, nondiscrimination in abortion decisions, and parental involvement for minors.

Document 1: Prohibits any person from performing or attempting to perform an abortion except in conformity with this Act's requirements.
Prohibits an abortion from being performed if the probable post-fertilization age of the unborn child is 20 weeks or greater.
Document 2: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion knowing that the abortion is sought based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child.
Authorizes civil actions, for verifiable money damages for injuries and punitive damages.
Document 3: Prohibits transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion.
Makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor.

topic.name: Consumer Protection in Transportation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers in transportation sectors, particularly regarding fees and labeling practices.

Document 1: prohibits an air carrier operating an aircraft from charging ticketed passengers a fee for the use of a lavatory
a carrier must refund any baggage fee for baggage that does not arrive within two hours
Document 2: deem a food to be misbranded if it contains genetically-engineered fish unless the food bears a label stating that fact
Document 3: Prohibits an air carrier from charging a fee for four or fewer bags checked by a member of the Armed Forces

topic.name: Elder Abuse Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing and preventing elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation through the establishment of offices, grant programs, and data collection efforts.

Document 1: Directs the Attorney General to study and report to Congress on state laws and practices relating to elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation
Authorizes appropriations for FY2009-FY2015.
Document 2: Establishes within the Department of Justice (DOJ) an Office of Elder Justice

Requires the Director of such Office to provide information, training, and technical assistance to assist states and local governments in preventing, investigating, prosecuting, and mitigating the impact of elder abuse.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension on Fibers and Footwear
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative amendments related to the temporary suspension or reduction of duties on specific fibers and footwear materials in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on staple fibers of rayon
presented in the form of top
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers
not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning
Document 3: extend temporarily the reduction of duty on certain footwear
with uppers of vegetable fibers

topic.name: Health Care Workforce Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to enhance training, diversity, and cultural competence within the health care workforce, particularly in relation to aging populations and health disparities.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to expand training for health professionals in the fields of geriatrics, long-term care, and chronic care management.
Requires an analysis of the health and long-term care workforce.
Document 2: HHS must engage in activities regarding health workforce diversity, including establishing a working group and a technical clearinghouse.
The bill sets forth provisions regarding cultural competence in federal health care programs and services.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and management of immigration processes and policies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to set forth provisions regarding the expedited removal of inadmissible arriving aliens.
Provisions regarding criminal aliens.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase criminal penalties for certain removed aliens who illegally reenter the United States.
Document 3: Makes 3,000 immigrant visas available for individuals who were born in Tibet and have been continuously residing in India or Nepal.

topic.name: Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at improving food safety and regulating harmful substances in food products.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act, the Poultry Products Inspection Act, and the Egg Products Inspection Act to include in the definition of 'adulterated' a product bearing or containing a pathogen or contaminant associated with serious illness or death.
Establishes penalties under the Acts for the introduction into commerce of unsafe or misbranded products of up to $100,000, 20 years' imprisonment, or both.
Document 2: This bill bans food containers composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA).
The FDA must promote and facilitate the use of BPA replacements.

topic.name: Gun Rights and Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss the enforcement and regulation of gun ownership and rights, particularly in relation to the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 2: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 3: District of Columbia House Voting Rights Act of 2009 - (Sec. 2) Considers the District of Columbia a congressional district for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives.
Declares that the District shall not be considered a state for purposes of representation in the Senate.

topic.name: Education Accountability Reforms
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving accountability and funding in K-12 education systems.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to exempt teachers who teach multiple subjects in certain small middle or secondary schools from being required to be highly qualified in each of their subjects
Allows states to use a growth model in calculating whether students are making adequately yearly progress (AYP) toward state academic performance standards
Document 2: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to condition local educational agency (LEA) receipt of school improvement funds on an average state and local spending per pupil
Requires annual LEA report cards to include certain information on state and local spending per pupil in schools
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states to exclude the results of limited English proficient students from adequate yearly progress (AYP) assessments

Gives schools which are identified as needing improvement, due to having failed for two consecutive years to make AYP, a full new school year to meet the standards before its students must be given the option to transfer

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy in Iraq and Syria
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative measures and strategies aimed at promoting stability, democratic transitions, and humanitarian assistance in Iraq and Syria.

Document 1: Directs the President to implement a regional diplomatic plan in conjunction with the U.N. Security Council to curtail the ongoing civil war in Iraq.

Directs the Secretary of State to organize an international donors conference to solicit additional international commitments of funding and support for reconstruction and economic development in Iraq.
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint a Special Envoy for Syria to coordinate U.S. engagement with the country and to ensure U.S. support for efforts to bring about a democratic transition in Syria.
Urges the United States to work with regional partners to secure conventional and unconventional weapons stockpiles in Syria and prevent their illicit sale or transfer out of Syria.
Document 3: Directs the President to report to Congress outlining a strategy to end U.S. combat operations in Iraq.
Authorizes the President to provide assistance to strengthen democracy, governance, human rights, the rule of law, and religious freedom in Iraq.

topic.name: Elementary and Secondary Education Reform
topic.description: Legislative reforms aimed at improving educational standards, funding, and accountability in K-12 education.

Document 1: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA)
Requires states to adopt college and career ready academic standards
Document 2: Reauthorizes through FY2019 the programs under titles I-IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA)
Gives states and LEAs flexibility in transferring funds among the programs under part A of title I
Document 3: Authorizes FY2014-FY2019 appropriations for the programs under titles I, II, III, and IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA)
Expresses the sense of Congress that states and local educational agencies (LEAs) should maintain the rights and responsibilities of determining educational curriculum

topic.name: U.S. Government Policy and Strategy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving the efficiency, competitiveness, and strategic planning of U.S. government agencies and sectors, particularly in manufacturing and homeland security.

Document 1: promote policies related to the nation's manufacturing sector
develop a Strategy
Document 2: examine the long-term global challenges facing the United States
develop legislative and administrative proposals to improve interagency cooperation
Document 3: assess the management structure and capabilities related to the Department of Homeland Security
make recommendations to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of DHS management

topic.name: Medicare Supervision Requirements
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative amendments to Medicare regulations concerning supervision requirements for radiology and therapeutic services.

Document 1: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise physician supervision requirements
aligns Medicare's requirements with state requirements
Document 2: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise physician supervision requirements
aligns Medicare's requirements with state requirements
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to state that, except with respect to high-risk or complex medical services
if the Secretary of Health and Human Services requires supervision by a physician or non-physician practitioner for Medicare payment

topic.name: Workplace Safety and Discrimination
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving workplace safety and preventing discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.
Document 2: Prohibits employers from adopting or implementing policies or practices that discourage or discriminate against employee reporting of work-related injuries or illnesses.
Declares OSHA inapplicable to working conditions covered by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977.
Document 3: Prohibits covered entities from engaging in employment discrimination on the basis of an individual's actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.
Declares that it shall be unlawful for an employer to discriminate with respect to the compensation, terms, conditions, or privileges of employment.

topic.name: FAA Funding and Programs
topic.description: Legislation related to the funding and operational programs of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).

Document 1: Reauthorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010-FY2012
Authorizes additional appropriations from the general fund of the Treasury for aviation programs through FY2012.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) out of the Airport and Airway Trust Fund for FY2012-FY2015
Requires the FAA Administrator to include in the Airway Capital Investment Plan a list of capital projects that are part of the Next Generation Air Transportation System (NextGen).
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010 and FY2011 for operations
Directs the FAA Administrator to establish a program to utilize undergraduate and technical colleges in research on subjects of relevance to the FAA.

topic.name: Public Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: The topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and use of public lands and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Tule Springs Fossil Beds National Monument consisting of approximately 22,650 acres of public land in Clark County, Nevada
Requires the Secretary to develop a management plan that provides for the long-term protection and management of the Monument.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to issue a special use authorization to each of the 20 owners of a water storage, transport, or diversion facility located on National Forest System land
Determined that the facility was in existence on the date on which the land upon which it is located was designated as part of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept any grazing lease that is voluntarily donated by a lessee with a valid existing grazing lease
Prohibits the establishment of any new allotments for livestock grazing that include any Monument land.

topic.name: Child Immigration and Citizenship
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation regarding the immigration status and citizenship rights of children, particularly focusing on unaccompanied minors and adopted children.

Document 1: Protection of Children Act of 2016 amends repatriation requirements for unaccompanied alien children.
An immigration officer shall return the child to his or her country of nationality or last habitual residence.
Document 2: Foreign Adopted Children Equality Act sets forth automatic citizenship criteria for children adopted outside the United States.
Deems such an adopted child to be a U.S. citizen at birth.

topic.name: Food Assistance and Nutrition Programs
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving food assistance programs and enhancing nutritional support for children and communities.

Document 1: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 regarding the supplemental food assistance program (SNAP)
Increases funding for community food projects and the seniors farmers' market nutrition program.
Document 2: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 regarding the supplemental food assistance program (SNAP)
Permits using benefits to purchase a community-supported agriculture share.
Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require the Secretary of Agriculture to provide performance awards to states
Establishes a demonstration project to determine the effectiveness of directly certifying children for free school meals using household income data from Medicaid.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving public health through disease prevention, research, and education.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee.
Directs the Committee to regularly review published public and private treatment guidelines.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop a multisite gestational diabetes research project.
Requires the Secretary to expand and intensify public health research on gestational diabetes.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to create the Blueprint for Health program to provide a national infrastructure for disease prevention.
Directs the Secretary to recommend to Congress legislation to implement the Blueprint for Health.

topic.name: Cloned Food Labeling Regulations
topic.description: Legislation addressing the labeling and regulation of food products derived from cloned animals to ensure consumer awareness and compliance.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act and the Federal Meat Inspection Act to deem as misbranded a food or meat food product that contains any part of a cloned animal and does not bear a conspicuous label.
Sets forth civil penalties for violation of this Act.
Document 2: Deems as misbranded a food that bears a label indicating that it does not contain cloned product, unless the label is in accordance with applicable regulations.
Allows a person to commence a civil action against a person who is alleged to have violated this Act.
Document 3: Consumer Right to Know Food Labeling Act of 2010 - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act and the Federal Meat Inspection Act.
Requires individuals to maintain a verifiable recordkeeping audit trail that will permit verification of compliance with this Act.

topic.name: Guantanamo Detention Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the detention and transfer of individuals held at Guantanamo Bay, focusing on restrictions and prohibitions on the use of funds for their release or transfer.

Document 1: prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States
No funds may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo
Document 2: prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States
No funds may be used to close or abandon Guantanamo
Document 3: prohibits U.S. agency funds from being used to transfer an individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay
No U.S. agency funds may be used to fund activities, envoys, or offices to carry out activities to close Guantanamo naval station

topic.name: Natural Gas Pipeline Regulation
topic.description: Legislation governing the authorization and management of natural gas pipelines on federal and public lands.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to grant a right of way for natural gas pipelines through a unit of the National Park System for a maximum term of 30 years.

Defines 'gas gathering line and associated field compression unit' as: (1) a pipeline installed to transport natural gas production associated with one or more wells drilled and completed to produce oil or gas.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to permit the use of rights of way for natural gas pipelines through public lands, forest, and other reservations of the United States.

Excludes from federal lands, for purposes of gas gathering line and associated field compression units, any unit of the National Park System, any unit of the National Wildlife Refuge System, or a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.

topic.name: Tax Credits for Alternative Fuels
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to tax incentives for the use and development of alternative fuel vehicles and infrastructure, particularly focusing on hydrogen and renewable energy sources.

Document 1: increases the rate of such credit from 30% to 50% for hydrogen-related alternative fuel vehicles
extend such credit through 2016 for property related to hydrogen.
Document 2: disallow the tax credit for renewable diesel used as fuel for any fuel derived from coprocessing biomass
amends the Internal Revenue Code to adjust renewable energy tax incentives.
Document 3: continue the maximum dollar amount (i.e., $8,000) of the tax credit for new qualified fuel cell motor vehicles
increases the amount of such credit for off-highway vehicles that achieve a specified level of electricity generation efficiency.

topic.name: Tariff Regulations and Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses the amendments and regulations related to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning various goods and their duty statuses.

Document 1: liquidate or reliquidate certain entries of large newspaper printing presses
components thereof
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
temporary suspension of duty on certain golf bags
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain cellular plastic sheets
for use in filters

topic.name: Trade and Communications Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the enforcement of trade laws and the regulation of communications, specifically focusing on textiles and television broadcasting.

Document 1: U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) should ensure that seizures, detentions, special operations, and Textile Product Verification Teams (TPVTs) remain the primary focus of their efforts to enforce U.S. customs laws with respect to imports of textile or apparel articles.

Requires the seizure and forfeiture of an imported textile or apparel article for which a trade preference has been claimed in cases where the importer has either misdescribed, not verified the article's country of origin, or used accompanying false documentation.
Document 2: Repeals provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 concerning the carriage of distant television stations and significantly viewed signals by satellite carriers.
Directs the FCC to repeal related FCC rules and eliminate restrictions on the number of broadcast television stations that a person or entity may own.

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures aimed at improving the accountability and transparency of federal regulatory processes.

Document 1: Defines 'major rule' and 'major guidance' for purposes of this Act as a rule or guidance that the Administrator of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) determines is likely to impose...
Revises procedures for rule making under the Administrative Procedure Act (APA) to require a federal agency...
Document 2: Defines 'major rule' and 'major guidance' for purposes of this Act as a rule or guidance that is likely to impose...
Revises procedures for rulemaking under the Administrative Procedure Act (APA) to require a federal agency...
Document 3: Requires the head of each federal agency to submit a monthly report to the Administrator of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB)...
Requires the Administrator to make such monthly reports publicly available on the Internet.

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses the revisions and reauthorizations of federal laws governing higher education institutions and student assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise and reauthorize HEA programs.
Revises the general definition of an institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that: (1) admit students whose secondary education was conducted in a home school setting.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific chemical compounds.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on 1-Oxa-3, 20-diazadispiro Y5.1.11.2? heneicosan-21-one 2,2,4,4-tetramethyl, reaction products with epichlorohydrin, hydrolyzed and polymerized.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on strontium halophosphate doped with europium.
suspension of duty on strontium halophosphate doped with europium
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on strontium halophosphate doped with europium.
suspension of duty on strontium halophosphate doped with europium

topic.name: Drug Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing the safety and integrity of prescription drugs to prevent abuse and ensure public health.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require any registered producer of a drug or device to notify the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS)...

Authorizes the Secretary to issue an order requiring any person who distributes a drug that may cause serious adverse health consequences or death to humans or animals to immediately cease distribution of such drug.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prescribe new drug application requirements for abuse-deterrent drugs...
Requires the Secretary to refuse a new drug application for any new (brand name) drug containing opium, an opiate, or a derivative as an active moiety that is not abuse-deterrent.

topic.name: Military Aircraft Travel Policy
topic.description: Policies regarding travel on military aircraft for former armed forces members with service-connected disabilities.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense to permit any former member of the armed forces who is entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability rated total by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to travel on military aircraft to the same extent as retired members of the armed forces.
entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability rated total
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense to permit any former member of the armed forces who is entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability rated total by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to travel on military aircraft to the same extent as retired members of the armed forces.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to permit any former member of the armed forces who is entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability rated total by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to travel on military aircraft to the same extent as retired members of the Armed Forces.

topic.name: Prescription Drug User Fees and Safety
topic.description: The documents discuss the legislative framework surrounding prescription drug user fees and the associated safety measures for drug approval and postmarket surveillance.

Document 1: Prescription Drug User Fee Amendments of 2007
postmarket safety activities within the process for the review of human drug applications
Document 2: authorized prescription drug fees be dedicated toward expediting the drug development process
postmarket safety activities within the process for the review of approved human drug applications

topic.name: Mortgage Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing assistance and alternatives to homeowners facing mortgage difficulties through various programs and refinancing options.

Document 1: Homeowner Foreclosure Reduction Act of 2016
requires certain nonprofit organizations and local governments to match the highest bids during sales of pools of certain nonperforming loans.
Document 2: Housing Opportunity and Mortgage Equity Act of 2013
providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages it owns.
Document 3: Principal Reduction Act of 2012
to reduce the outstanding principal balances on qualified mortgages on single-family housing they own or guarantee.

topic.name: Water Resources Development Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to the development, management, and improvement of water resources in the United States.

Document 1: authorize projects that are the subject of a completed report of the Chief of Engineers
authorize the Secretary to carry out projects for water resources development, conservation, and other purposes
Document 2: amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1986
the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to provide technical assistance to nonfederal interests
Document 3: requires the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to enter cooperative agreements with youth service and conservation corps organizations
makes permanent a requirement under WRDA 1986 that the Corps of Engineers use at least 10% of the difference between the funds made available for a fiscal year

topic.name: Housing and Financial Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to housing and financial support for individuals and families.

Document 1: Preventing Mortgage Foreclosures - Helping Families Save Their Homes Act of 2009
engage in loss mitigation actions as an alternative to foreclosure
Document 2: FHA Modernization Act of 2007 - Building American Homeownership
modify requirements for the maximum principal loan obligation
Document 3: Empowering Students Through Enhanced Financial Counseling Act
ensure that individuals who receive a Federal Pell Grant or a William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan receive comprehensive counseling

topic.name: Indian Health Care Improvement
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing health care services and policies for Indian populations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to authorize appropriations to carry out the Act.
Establishes an Indian Catastrophic Health Emergency Fund (CHEF) to meet extraordinary medical costs.
Document 2: Amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to declare a new national Indian health policy.
Revises Indian Health Scholarship requirements.
Document 3: Reauthorizes Indian Health Service (IHS) programs.
Ensures maximum Indian participation in the direction of health care services.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Appropriations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments and appropriations related to specific programs and funding initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Afghan Allies Protection Act of 2009
increase to 4,000 the number of Afghan special immigrant visas
Document 2: Amends the Soda Ash Royalty Reduction Act of 2006
extend the reduced (2%) royalty rate for soda ash
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
Authorizes appropriations

topic.name: Higher Education Funding Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts aimed at enhancing funding and support for specific educational institutions and programs, particularly for underrepresented and nursing education.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to provide grants to Predominantly Black Institutions
enhance their capacity to serve more low- and middle-income Black American students
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to include accelerated degree nursing students
Raises the yearly loan amounts available to all nursing students through that Program
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to include accelerated degree nursing students
Directs the Secretary to award grants to eligible entities to establish doctoral nursing degree programs

topic.name: Pipeline and Environmental Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and environmental protection in pipeline transportation and related maritime operations.

Document 1: Prescribes or revises requirements for safety and environmental protection in pipeline transportation.
Subjects to a civil penalty of $250,000 any person that the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) has found to have committed a major consequence violation.
Document 2: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 (OPA) to repeal the limitation on the liability of the responsible party for an offshore facility for damages and removal costs.
Increases the minimum amount of financial responsibility required for offshore facilities to $1.5 billion.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to allow a person owning, operating, or chartering a commercial vessel to discharge ballast water into navigable waters only if the discharge meets the ballast water performance standard.
Defines 'commercial vessel' to mean a watercraft or other artificial contrivance used or capable of being used as a means of transportation on water that is engaged in commercial service.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Indian Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the federal recognition of the Chickahominy Indian Tribe in Virginia and the implications of such recognition.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits.

topic.name: Violence Against Women Act
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing protections and services for victims of violence against women, including definitions, grant conditions, and support for culturally specific services.

Document 1: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 (VAWA) to add or expand definitions of several terms
Expands the purposes for which STOP grants may be used to include training of law enforcement personnel and prosecutors
Document 2: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 (VAWA) to add or expand definitions of several terms
Requires the Office on Violence Against Women of the Department of Justice (DOJ) to establish a biennial conferral process
Document 3: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 (VAWA) to add or expand definitions of several terms
Modifies or expands grant conditions under such Act, including requirements relating to nondisclosure of personally identifying information

topic.name: School Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety and security in public schools through measures such as transferring students from unsafe schools and implementing background checks for school personnel.

Document 1: require states to allow a student who is attending a public school that does not have a safe climate for academic achievement to transfer to a safe public school
requires the counseling or removal of offenders
Document 2: requires states to permit schools to provide counseling and educational services to violent offenders in alternative settings
require schools that know a violent offender is returning to school to notify the victim's parents
Document 3: require each state and local educational agency (LEA) that receives funds under the ESEA to have policies and procedures in effect that require a criminal background check for each school employee
authorize states or LEAs to use ESEA funds to establish, implement, or improve policies and procedures on background checks for school employees

topic.name: Medicare Provider Access Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at expanding access to hospice and rehabilitation services under Medicare by recognizing additional healthcare providers.

Document 1: Medicare Hospice Care Access Act of 2013
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians to serve hospice patients.
