Document 2: Autonomy for Psychologists and Social Workers Act of 2009
remove the restriction that a clinical psychologist or clinical social worker provide services in a comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation facility to a patient only under the care of a physician.
Document 3: Medicare Hospice Care Access Act of 2011
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians to serve hospice patients.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Climate Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative efforts related to energy production, environmental protection, and greenhouse gas emission regulations.

Document 1: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing or leasing of any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) that was not available for oil and gas leasing as of July 1, 2008
Requires approval by law of an adjacent state for oil or gas leasing in any area located within 100 miles from the adjacent state's coastline.
Document 2: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG, including stationary combustion source emissions from combustion of fuels in stationary equipment.
Document 3: Sets forth provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution
Establishing a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions and setting goals for reducing such emissions from covered sources by 83% of 2005 levels by 2050.

topic.name: Housing Finance Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss the regulation and reform of housing finance entities, primarily focusing on Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, and the establishment of regulatory authorities to ensure financial stability and oversight.

Document 1: establish a Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA)
supervisory and regulatory authority over Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and federal home loan banks
Document 2: establishes the FMIC as an independent agency
dissolve and eliminate Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac
Document 3: establishes the Financial Stability Oversight Council
identify risks to U.S. financial stability

topic.name: Renewable Fuel Policy Changes
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Clean Air Act affecting renewable fuel requirements and environmental regulations.

Document 1: This bill amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program by decreasing the volume of renewable fuel that must be contained in motor vehicle fuel in 2015 through 2022 to 7.5 billion gallons for each year.
The bill revokes the requirement that the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) ensure that renewable fuels emit fewer greenhouse gases than the fuel it replaces.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act, with respect to reductions in requirements to use cellulosic biofuel under the renewable fuel program.
Revises cellulosic biofuel use requirements to require the Administrator to reduce the applicable volume of renewable fuel and advanced biofuels requirement.

topic.name: Military Discharge and Family Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses the regulations and assistance related to military discharges, mental health considerations, and family support for service members and veterans.

Document 1: each member of the Armed Forces being considered for any type of military discharge to receive written notice
Prohibits the Secretary of a military department from discharging for a personality disorder a member unless the member has undergone testing
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of each military department to provide each reserve member of that department who is being evaluated for separation or retirement for disability
Requires military human resources commands to return to active duty during their evaluation period reserve members who request such option
Document 3: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to revise its requirements for exigency leave with respect to employees belonging to the family of members of the Armed Forces
Entitles to coverage by the Act an eligible employee who is a family member caring for a veteran undergoing medical treatment

topic.name: HIV/AIDS Prevention in African-American Communities
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reducing HIV/AIDS through targeted outreach, prevention, and support services for the African-American community.

Document 1: Authorize the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities to reduce HIV/AIDS in the African-American community.
Expand and intensify HIV/AIDS activities in African-American communities.
Document 2: Authorize the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to make grants to faith-based organizations for public health intervention and prevention activities in the African-American community to reduce HIV/AIDS.
Implement a national media outreach campaign to urge sexually active individuals to be tested for and know their HIV/AIDS status.
Document 3: Authorize the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to provide family reunification services, mental health counseling, HIV/AIDS, sexually transmitted disease, and substance abuse testing and treatment to runaway or homeless youth or youth who are HIV positive or at risk for HIV/AIDS.
Research strategies to reduce the transmission of HIV/AIDS in the African-American community.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care, benefits, and support systems for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Extends the period during which additional dependency and indemnity compensation (DIC) will be paid to surviving spouses with children.
Expands the Marine Gunnery Sergeant John David Fry Scholarship to include surviving spouses of service members who die in the line of duty.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to waive the imposition or collection of copayments for telehealth and telemedicine visits of veterans.
Directs the Secretary to carry out a program of teleconsultation for the provision of remote mental health assessments.
Document 3: Directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to jointly develop and implement a comprehensive policy on the care and management of members of the Armed Forces.
Requires the policy developed to include mechanisms to ensure responsibility for recovering members in medical hold or holdover status.

topic.name: Support for Military Personnel
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at providing financial support and health services to military personnel and their families.

Document 1: maximum monthly rates of hostile fire or imminent danger pay
monthly family separation allowance
Document 2: appropriations for diabetes research
diabetes prevention and treatment through Indian health facilities
Document 3: designate a portion of any income tax overpayment
benefit reservists and National Guard members

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and Border Security
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts focused on immigration policies, border security measures, and the integration of immigrants into American society.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from implementing the New Worker Program established in title IV or grant conditional nonimmigrant classification under title VI of this Act prior to certification to the President and to Congress that specified conditions have been met respecting: (1) border security; (2) document security;
Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions, including provisions respecting: (1) personnel and asset increases and enhancements; (2) a National Strategy for Border Security;
Document 2: Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for: (1) migration and refugee assistance; and (2) refugee resettlement in Israel.
Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for the Department of State (Department) to carry diplomatic and consular affairs, including: (1) worldwide security protection; (2) public diplomacy;
Document 3: States that the Department of Homeland Security's (DHS) border security goal is to gain situational awareness and operational control of high traffic areas and operational control along the Southwest border of the United States.

Prohibits the Secretary from processing applications for registered provisional immigrant status (RPI) until the Secretary has submitted to Congress the notice of commencement of implementation of the Comprehensive Southern Border Security Strategy.

topic.name: Election Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign financing and ensuring transparency in political spending during elections.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements and benefits of fair elections financing of Senate election campaigns
establishment of a Fair Elections Fund
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit independent expenditures and payments for electioneering communications by government contractors
Applies the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to foreign-controlled domestic corporations
Document 3: Prohibits independent expenditures and payments for electioneering communications by government contractors if the value of the contract is at least $10 million
Requires the highest ranking official of a corporation to file a certification with the Federal Election Commission before making any contribution or expenditure

topic.name: Fair Share Tax Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring high-income taxpayers contribute a minimum tax rate to promote tax equity and reform the tax system.

Document 1: require an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%
expresses the sense of the Senate that Congress should enact tax reform that repeals unfair and unnecessary tax loopholes
Document 2: require an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%
expresses the sense of the House of Representatives that Congress should enact tax reform that repeals unfair and unnecessary tax loopholes
Document 3: require an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%
expresses the sense of the House of Representatives that Congress should enact tax reform that repeals unfair and unnecessary tax loopholes

topic.name: Federal Designations and Honors
topic.description: This topic encompasses the formal renaming and designation of federal buildings and facilities to honor individuals or significant contributions.

Document 1: Redesignates the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Hugh L. Dryden Flight Research Center as the 'NASA Neil A. Armstrong Flight Research Center'
Redesignates the NASA Western Aeronautical Test Range as the 'NASA Hugh L. Dryden Aeronautical Test Range'
Document 2: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs community-based outpatient clinic as the 'Loren R. Kaufman Memorial Veterans' Clinic'
Document 3: Redesignates the Department of Labor building as the 'Reed Larson Building'

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Capital Requirements
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative acts aimed at regulating banking practices and establishing capital requirements for financial institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States.
Prescribes leverage ratio requirements for operating subsidiaries of bank holding companies and financial companies.
Document 2: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements on a consolidated basis.

States that federal banking agencies shall not be required to subject any person to such minimum capital requirements, to the extent that such person either acts in its capacity as a regulated insurance entity.

topic.name: Medicare Legislation for Patients
topic.description: Legislation related to Medicare coverage and policies affecting patients, particularly those with kidney transplants and identity protection measures.

Document 1: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Benefits) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to extend the months of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs for kidney transplant patients.
Amends SSA title XVIII (Medicare) to make eligible for enrollment in Medicare part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance), solely for the purpose of such drug coverage.
Document 2: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Benefits) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to extend the months of coverage of immunosuppressive drugs for kidney transplant patients.
Amends SSA title XVIII (Medicare) to make eligible for enrollment in Medicare part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance), solely for the purpose of such drug coverage.
Document 3: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish cost-effective procedures to ensure that: (1) a Social Security account number (or any derivative) is not displayed, coded, or embedded on the Medicare card.
any other identifier displayed on such card is not identifiable as a Social Security account number (or any derivative).
